artifacts.role: generic PHP-core IR printer now routing instance methods through adapter plans and printing bool literals
typed shell metadata for the grouped WP_Http block_request adapter
grouped WP_Http helper compiler manifest proving fourteen adapter cases
WPHX-312.54 oracle/candidate manifest using the compiler-emitted original-path public shell
behavior receipt for the retired temporary shell candidate

claims: WPHX PHP emits the original-path WP_Http::block_request instance adapter in the grouped class-wp-http.php shell.
The WPHX-312.54 candidate no longer copies and patches a PHP method body in its runner.

PHP still owns WP_HTTP_BLOCK_EXTERNAL, WP_ACCESSIBLE_HOSTS, parse_url, get_option('siteurl'), and block_local_requests filter timing at the public boundary while Haxe owns localhost/site-host and accessible-host matching policy helpers.
non_claims: This does not claim whole-file WP_Http ownership.
This does not claim WP_Http::request, live HTTP transport, Requests network I/O, DNS/proxy/TLS behavior, installed distribution behavior, or generated WP_Http_Cookie ownership.
This does not claim WPHX PHP is a complete arbitrary-Haxe PHP backend.
